mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5%, labor market deterioration signals impending economic contraction, causing investors to discount future corporate earnings and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of instances where UNRATE crosses 5.5%, or signal loses predictive power (p-value > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a statistically significant positive return differential (t-stat < 1.96) compared to periods with divergent retail and housing data.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices indicate strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying in equities and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in >60% of observations during recovery phases, or the 5-day return correlation with INDPRO growth falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >70% of months with CPIAUCSL acceleration, or the inflation-return correlation remains statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll employment decline exceeding 100,000 signals severe labor market weakness and reduced consumer spending capacity, leading to downward revisions in earnings forecasts and negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by more than 100,000 in a single month
failure_condition: The threshold signal yields positive forward returns in >55% of triggered cases, or the predictive accuracy does not exceed random chance in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP expansion and stable or declining federal funds rates create an optimal growth environment that supports corporate profitability and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 2% annually, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during periods of monetary tightening reflects supply-side constraints and weakening economic momentum, prompting risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of observations when INDPRO falls during tightening phases, or the signal's Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5 in out-of-sample tests.
